LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BY DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGUARD INSURANCE CO.'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS GROUNDS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. (SEE, E.G., FAC 21-22.). A FORMAL ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER KENDRICK AND GAMIN CIRCE IS SUSTAINED WITH 21 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD MORE ULTIMATE FACTS TO SUPPORT THEIR CLAIMS FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BAD FAITH. (CONSTRUCTION PROTECTIVE SERVICES, INC. V. TIG SPECIALTY INS. CO. (2002) 29 CAL.4TH 189, 198-199; CROSKEY, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: INSURANCE LITIGATION (THE RUTTER GROUP 2017) PAR. 15:56.) THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT DESCRIBE THE POLICY PERIOD OR WHEN OR HOW THE LOSS OCCURRED, AND ONLY GENERALLY DESCRIBES THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Trial By Defendant Cse Safegaurd Insurance Company
Off Calendar Aug-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Bifurcate Trial By Defendant Cse Safegaurd Insurance Company.

Defendant CSE Safeguard Insurance Co.'s "motion to bifurcate trial" is denied without prejudice. Issues of severance and trial order are reserved for the trial judge.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint By Defendant Cse Safeguard Insurance Company
Off Calendar Nov-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. KELLY M. MANNION, ESQ. of Mannion Lowe & Oksenedler (Tel: 415-733-1050) appearing for plaintiffs Christopher Kendrick and Gamin Circe;
Atty. TIMOTHY S. KIRK, ESQ. of Medina Seto Law Group (Tel: 415-851-9868) appearing for Defendant CSE Safeguard Insurance Company.

DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint By Defendant Cse Safeguard Insurance Company

The matter was argued and submitted by counsel for both sides. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant CSE Safeguard Insurance Co.'s motion to strike is DENIED. The first amended complaint adequately pleads grounds for punitive damages. (See, e.g., FAC 21-22.). A formal order is signed in open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Continued To Dec-26-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Continued To Nov-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Kelly M. Mannion of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler, Tel. No. 1-415-733-0066
appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Rowena Seto, Tel. No. 1-415-851-9887 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant CSE Safeguard Insurance Company's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Christopher Kendrick and Gamin Circe is sustained with 21 days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead more ultimate facts to support their claims for breach of contract and bad faith. (Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198-199; Croskey, Cal. Practice Guide: Insurance Litigation (The Rutter Group 2017) par. 15:56.) The complaint does not adequately plead a breach of contract claim. The complaint does not describe the policy period or when or how the loss occurred, and only generally describes the terms of the contract. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Kelly M. Mannion of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler, Tel. No. 1-415-733-0066
appearing for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Rowena Seto, Tel. No. 1-415-851-9887 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGAURD INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT - Defendant CSE Safeguard Insurance Company's motion to strike the demand for punitive damages in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Christopher Kendrick and Gamin Circe is off calendar in light of the ruling on CSE's demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
